figures.caption: FIG. 3. (a) σ1(ω) spectra (circles) at temperatures of 10, 100, 200, and 370 K in the ℏω region below 150 meV and the fitting curves with three peaks at about 17, 20, and 60 meV at 10 K. (b) Fano asymmetric parameter (1/q2, red solid circles) of the phonon peak and the peak intensity (N∗, blue open squares) of the 20-meV peak as a function of temperature. (c) Expected temperature-dependent electronic-structure change near EF and the origins of the peaks in σ1(ω) spectra.

FIG. 2. (a) Temperature-dependent R(ω) spectra near the energy gap. Each spectrum is vertically shifted by 0.02 as the temperature increases. As shown in Fig. 1(a), interferences are visible below the energy gap, and ends of the interference (marked by down arrows, evaluated as the crossing point of the envelop lines as shown at T = 150 K) correspond to the onset of the energy gap (Eg). (b) Temperature-dependent σ1(ω) spectra in the ℏω region below 300 meV. (c) Eg (red solid circles) and the effective electron number (N∗, blue open squares) evaluated in the ℏω range of 20–200 meV as a function of temperature, accompanied by the square of magnetization [M(T )2, thick dashed line] expected with the Mn2+ 3d5 high-spin state (J = 5/2). TN is shown by a vertical arrow.
